prd_ref: PRD §6 Shared-Device False Positive / §7 Eval E
title: Shared-device household — false-positive trap
fixture_note: Operator-facing demo-case scenario. Structure + entity references only — NO computed scores or classifications (P3 produces those). This is a deliberate FALSE-POSITIVE TRAP: a shared device exists, but every corroborating collusion signal is absent. Correct outcome is MONITOR ONLY — no suppression, no enforcement.
narrative: Household members H1 (P-192) and H2 (P-193) share a device (DG-002) and a household (H-01) — a single weak signal that, in isolation, can look like the cluster case. But the corroborating signals that make CASE-C suspicious are all ABSENT here: co-seating is rare (2 of 22, 0.091), session-time preferences differ (H1 evening / H2 morning-afternoon), playing styles differ (VPIP 0.27 vs 0.35; PFR 0.20 vs 0.15), session lengths differ by ~35 min, soft_play_delta is 0.00 for both, there's no correlated profitability and no casual-exit increase. A system that escalates this is over-triggering on the device link alone. Correct action: MONITOR ONLY.

demo_spine.lobby: Player lobby unaffected — nothing integrity-related shown player-side.
demo_spine.pit_boss: Operator console shows the same-device flag PLUS the full set of exculpatory signals as counter-evidence. AI summary must NOT state collusion and must recommend monitor, not escalate.
demo_spine.simulator: Standard: H1/H2 play normally, no intervention needed. FairPlay: H1/H2 monitored only — the system correctly AVOIDS a false positive.
demo_spine.eval: Eval E checks monitor-only (no suppression/enforcement), exculpatory signals surfaced as counter-evidence, and that the AI does not state collusion as fact.

divergence_points.standard: No action on the H1/H2 same-device flag (none surfaced)
divergence_points.fairplay: Monitor only — same device but no co-seating/soft-play, so do not escalate

expected_outcome.standard: H1/H2 play normally; no suppression needed; no metric impact (they are not a health or integrity problem).
expected_outcome.fairplay: H1/H2 monitored only; correctly NOT counted as a high-risk formation — this is why fairplay high_risk_seating_formations stays at 1 (the contained cluster), not inflated by this household.

metrics_ref.note: This case is a NON-event in the metrics by design — its correctness shows up as the ABSENCE of an extra high-risk formation.
metrics_ref.assertion: data/room_metrics_fairplay.json hour 8 high_risk_seating_formations == 1 (the contained cluster only) — the monitored household is NOT counted as a formation.

exculpatory_signals.note: Simulated ground-truth seeds from seeded_case_labels.json. P3 computes the real packet; these are the demo target.

counter_evidence: Same device is expected for a household — not evidence of coordinated play.
Low co-seating (0.091) directly contradicts a coordination hypothesis.
Different session times, styles, and lengths argue against shared strategy.
Zero soft-play delta — no value being passed between the accounts.
Recommended action is MONITOR — not suppression, not enforcement.
lens_separation_guard: This case is a FALSE_POSITIVE trap that probes the INTEGRITY lens. It MUST rank BELOW the true integrity case (CASE-C) and must NOT be escalated. Conflating this household with CASE-C's coordinated cluster — treating a shared device as proof of collusion — is a failing eval. The contrast pair CASE-C (escalate to review) vs CASE-E (monitor only) is the core demonstration that the system separates real integrity risk from innocent linkage.
id_reconciliation: All IDs (P-192, P-193, DG-002, H-01) match across players.json, relationships.json, and seeded_case_labels.json. No conflicts.
